title: Open Talent Protocol
description: An open, machine-first standard for representing a person's professional profile in agentic talent marketplaces. v0.1.

verificationObject.description: Provenance and verification record attached to an individual data item.

level.description: How this item was verified. 'self' = self-reported, 'test' = passed an assessment, 'issuer' = confirmed by the credential issuer, 'platform' = verified by the hosting platform.

issuerName.description: Name of the entity that performed or attests to the verification.

url.description: Link to the verification artifact or badge.

visibilityLevel.description: Audience allowed to see this data. 'public' = anyone, 'recruiters' = authenticated recruiters/employers, 'private' = owner only.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the Open Talent Protocol schema this document conforms to.

language.description: Primary natural language of the document content.

lastUpdated.description: When this document was last modified.

source.description: System or actor that last wrote this document (e.g. 'user', 'jobgrow', 'import:jsonresume', 'agent').

dataController.description: The legal data controller responsible for this document — the entity that determines the purposes and means of processing the personal data it contains. May be an individual (the subject themselves), an employer, or a platform. Can be a name, a URI, or a platform-specific identifier. Relevant for GDPR Article 4(7) compliance and similar data protection frameworks.

subjectId.description: A stable identifier for the human subject of this document. This is NOT necessarily an email address; it may be a platform-assigned ID, a DID (Decentralized Identifier), or any other persistent, portable identifier the subject controls. Intended to enable de-duplication, agent ownership, and future verifiable credential binding without prescribing an identity regime.

preferredName.description: Name the person prefers to be called (first name or nickname).

phone.description: Phone number in E.164 or local format.

location.description: Geographic location, used for matching and display.

profiles.description: Public online profiles (GitHub, LinkedIn, personal site, etc.).

description.description: Free-form description (e.g. 'EU citizen, no sponsorship required').

authorizedCountries.description: Countries where the person is currently authorized to work.

requiresSponsorship.description: Whether the person requires visa sponsorship to work in countries not listed above.

summary.description: A short professional summary or elevator pitch written in first or third person. Plain text only; no markup.

work.description: Chronological work history, most recent first.

current.description: Set to true if this is the current position. If true, endDate should be omitted.

highlights.description: Bullet-point accomplishments and responsibilities. Plain text.

impact.description: Quantified impact statements for agent-driven ranking and comparison.

metric.description: Name of the metric (e.g. 'revenue growth', 'team size', 'uptime improvement').

value.description: The measured value as a human-readable string (e.g. '+35%', '€2M ARR', '12 engineers').

period.description: Time period over which the metric was achieved (e.g. 'FY2023', '18 months').

tags.description: Free-form domain or technology tags to aid search and matching.

degree.description: Degree type (e.g. 'Bachelor of Science', 'Master of Arts', 'Ausbildung').

grade.description: Final grade or GPA in the institution's own notation.

highlights.description: Notable achievements, thesis title, clubs, etc.

skills.description: Normalized skill inventory. Intended to be machine-readable and matchable across profiles.

category.description: Skill category for grouping (e.g. 'programming language', 'framework', 'soft skill', 'clinical').

lastUsed.description: Date when this skill was last actively used.

yearsOfExperience.description: Approximate years of practical experience with this skill.

projects.description: Selected personal, open-source, or professional projects that demonstrate skills.

description.description: What the project does and why it matters. Plain text.

role.description: The person's role in the project (e.g. 'Lead Developer', 'Contributor').

credentials.description: Degrees, professional certifications, and regulated licenses.

name.description: Credential name (e.g. 'AWS Certified Solutions Architect', 'RN License').

credentialId.description: Certificate or license ID number, if publicly verifiable.

url.description: Link to the credential record or badge.

level.description: Proficiency level using CEFR scale, or 'native'.

preferences.description: Candidate's job search preferences. This section is first-class because it is the primary input for agentic matching — agents read this to determine fit before comparing work history.

desiredRoles.description: Job titles or role types the candidate is actively seeking.

industries.description: Industries or verticals of interest. Optional.

locations.description: Preferred cities, regions, or countries. Use country codes or plain city names.

period.description: Pay period for the stated amounts.

constraints.description: Non-negotiable conditions and preferences that agents must respect.

noticePeriod.description: Current notice period or availability (e.g. '4 weeks', 'immediately').

travelWillingness.description: Willingness to travel for the role.

notes.description: Free-form notes on non-negotiables (e.g. 'no on-call rotations', 'school hours only').

evidence.description: Reusable portfolio artifacts and proof-of-work items that support claims made elsewhere in the document.

relatedSkills.description: Names of skills (from the 'skills' array) that this artifact demonstrates.

issuer.description: Platform or organization that hosts or issued this artifact (e.g. 'GitHub', 'Medium', 'Codility').

createdBy.description: Who or what originally created this document.

updatedBy.description: Actor that last updated the document (e.g. 'user', 'agent:jobgrow-v2', 'import:jsonresume').

integrity.description: Content hash of the document (excluding this field) for tamper detection. Format: '<algorithm>:<hex-digest>' (e.g. 'sha256:abc123...'). v0.1 treats this as informational only.

visibility.description: Privacy controls governing who can read sections or items in this document.

defaultVisibility.description: Baseline visibility for the entire document. Overrides apply on top of this.

overrides.description: Per-section or per-item visibility overrides. Each entry maps a JSON Pointer path to a visibility level.

path.description: JSON Pointer (RFC 6901) to the field or array item being overridden (e.g. '/identity/contact/phone', '/work/0/impact').

custom.description: Extension namespace for domain-specific or proprietary fields. Implementors should namespace their keys (e.g. 'jobgrow:*', 'myapp:*') to avoid collisions. The schema places no constraints on this object's contents.
